Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

HC + Pods are OK but isn't a bit too late for El Alto?

What is the rationale of this (https://jira.onap.org/browse/POLICY-2171 is not really helpful...)?

Patch Set 1:

Hi Sylvain, it was considered that would be good to have if there was  a new el alto maintenance release made available, that's the only reason, as the most recent version was not correct in el alto charts.  Nevertheless, not aware of side effects with current settings, although it would be best just in case to point to the right versions in El Alto.

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1
> 
> HC + Pods are OK but isn't a bit too late for El Alto?
> 
> What is the rationale of this (https://jira.onap.org/browse/POLICY-2171 is not really helpful...)?

Not too late - the component is our legacy component and it was not part of Integration testing. Subsequent testing revealed that these fields were not updated. Teams that were still using legacy will need this. As Jorge indicated, its best to point to the correct released versions.

Patch Set 1: Code-Review+1

Hi Sylvain - There is currently no official El Alto maintenance release. Nevertheless Frankfurt is now planned in May 2020.

I think we should provide the opportunity to teams to self-release based on issues or feedback they are getting from the Community.
It will also allow us to move to a more Agile way.
We defined the following process - https://wiki.onap.org/display/DW/ONAP+Maintenance+Policy.
I think we need to define a tag to avoid any confusion with the first official El Alto release. Maybe we should move this discussion to mailing list?

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1: Code-Review+1
> 
> Hi Sylvain - There is currently no official El Alto maintenance release. Nevertheless Frankfurt is now planned in May 2020.
> 
> I think we should provide the opportunity to teams to self-release based on issues or feedback they are getting from the Community.
> It will also allow us to move to a more Agile way.
> We defined the following process - https://wiki.onap.org/display/DW/ONAP+Maintenance+Policy.
> I think we need to define a tag to avoid any confusion with the first official El Alto release. Maybe we should move this discussion to mailing list?

elalto should be tagged already. So people can pull the exact chart tagged for elalto release. But they should also have the option to pull the updated charts due to simple fixes done by teams that do not require a full maintenance release by ONAP.

Patch Set 1:

The elalto tag is really 5.0.1-ONAP on the elalto branch. A point release of elalto would be 5.0.2-ONAP tag on the elalto branch.

Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

It looks like this change is never going to be merged into elalto. Please abandon

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1
> 
> It looks like this change is never going to be merged into elalto. Please abandon

It should be.
